data lake migration failure.title: Feature Store Architecture: Fix Training-Serving Skew | by Armin Norouzi, Ph.D | Jun, 2026 | AI Advances - Medium
data lake migration failure.text: Feature Store Architecture: Fix Training-Serving Skew | by Armin Norouzi, Ph.D | Jun, 2026 | AI Advances  Medium

summary: Article discusses the issue of training-serving skew in feature stores, which can lead to inconsistent model performance between training and serving environments.
The author proposes a feature store architecture that addresses this issue by using a unified data model, data versioning, and a caching layer.
The article also highlights the importance of data governance and data quality in feature stores.
analysis.pain_point: Training-serving skew in feature stores, inconsistent model performance between training and serving environments
analysis.zetaris_relevance: Low relevance — The article focuses on feature store architecture and training-serving skew, which is not directly related to Zetaris's domain of federated query, semantic layer, data silo break-up, data integration for AI, governance across heterogeneous sources, lakehouse modernisation, regulatory lineage, M&A consolidation, data mesh / data product programme.
